description: Immutable append-only event graph forming a room's history and derived state. Handles state resolution on conflicts, efficient state caching, and authorization at every event.

actors.name: Homeserver
actors.description: Server persisting events and maintaining state

actors.name: Remote Server
actors.description: Foreign server sending events via federation

fields.name: event_id
fields.label: Globally unique immutable identifier for an event

fields.name: event_type
fields.label: Type identifier distinguishing state events from timeline events

fields.name: state_key
fields.label: Secondary key; combined with event_type to define a unique state slot

fields.name: content
fields.label: Payload of the event; interpreted according to event_type

fields.name: depth
fields.label: Integer DAG depth for topological ordering

fields.name: auth_event_ids
fields.label: State events that cryptographically authorize this event

fields.name: state_group
fields.label: Numeric identifier for a resolved state snapshot stored in the database

fields.name: origin_server_ts
fields.label: Timestamp set by the originating server at event creation

values.description: State snapshot is loaded in memory cache
State is being fetched from persistent storage
Cache entry expired after 60 minutes of inactivity

transitions.description: State required for event processing; reload from storage triggered
State loaded from database and placed back in cache

immutability: Events are immutable once persisted; corrections are made only through redaction events
Every event must declare its auth events; the auth chain must be closed
State is tracked per (event_type, state_key) pair; only the most recent event per slot is current state
The genesis event must have no predecessor events and must not specify a room identifier in its content
Genesis events must declare a creator
validation: All events must pass cryptographic signature validation before persistence
Events may not exceed the maximum permitted size
State resolution is applied when concurrent events create conflicting state; algorithm depends on room version
caching: State cache entries expire after 60 minutes; long-idle rooms reload state from persistent storage on demand
State queries may be filtered to a subset of event types to reduce load

given: event passes authorization rules for its type and state_key
event size does not exceed the maximum limit
auth event chain is valid and closed

then.description: Event appended to the room's persistent event graph
New state group assigned if event modifies state

event_accepted.result: Event is part of the room history and state is updated if applicable

given: concurrent events modify the same state slot
state resolution algorithm produces a deterministic winner

then.description: Resolved state snapshot stored with new state group identifier

state_conflict_resolved.result: Single authoritative current state established despite concurrent edits

any: event auth chain contains an invalid reference
event sender lacks permission for this event type
event violates a room version-specific rule

given: event is missing a valid server signature
event content exceeds the maximum allowed byte size

errors.message: Event rejected due to invalid or missing signatures
Event exceeds the maximum permitted size
Event is missing required fields or contains invalid structure

events.name: room.event.persisted
events.description: An event has been validated and appended to the room graph

events.name: room.state.resolved
events.description: State conflict resolved; new authoritative state snapshot stored

events.name: room.event.rejected
events.description: An event was rejected due to auth, signature, or size failure

related.reason: Power level state event is consulted during every state event authorization
Federation sends and receives events; state resolution applies to federated events
Room creation writes the genesis event that anchors the entire state graph
Redaction prunes event content while preserving its position in the graph
